<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We can take delight in the fact that, yes, Jesus is everywhere, guiding and modeling community for us.<|12.22|> <|13.10|> Jesus is present with <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As we enter into this new year, into this season of Advent, and as we struggle to do Advent justice in a culture bent on zooming straight to Daily Examine.<|27.16|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And as I look out at you and see my spiritual director in the crowd, I'm sure she finds this very amusing that I'm going to do five-step process.<|10.36|> <|10.86|> And I'm really going to do this.<|12.12|> <|12.46|> And I want you all to hold me accountable.<|13.88|> <|13.88|> And if you try it, check in with me.<|17.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The first is stillness, relaxing in God's presence in a favorite prayer place or posture.<|8.54|> <|9.04|> The second is gratitude, reviewing our day and giving thanks to God for the simple gifts,<|16.80|> <|17.54|> the smell of coffee, the smile of a friend, the incredible beauty of the mountains as we turn west on a street.<|26.80|> of the church, this time for reflection and preparation,<|23.96|> <|23.96|> we're going to be a little bit more.<|24.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The king we receive in Jesus <|10.10|> To give thanks to God for the grace that allows us to get up tomorrow<|14.48|>
<|14.48|> and do it all over again.<|16.50|>
<|17.18|> Have another chance to hopefully have more sheep moments than goat moments.<|23.58|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To give us another chance to see and serve Jesus in every person<|5.36|>
<|5.36|> and build community by caring for all<|10.44|>
<|10.44|> and by seeing others in their complexity, their messiness,<|15.64|>
<|16.18|> their humanity, and their divinity.<|18.28|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When we reflect on the day's activities, hopefully we will see God's invitation to us and our response as sheep or a lack of response as goats.<|13.08|>
<|15.22|> On this Christ the King Sunday, this New Year's Eve of the church, this time for reflection and preparation,<|23.96|>
<|23.96|> we're going to be a little bit more.<|24.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The king we receive in Jesus is not the earthly king of jeweled crowns, distance, aloofness, riches, and an ornate throne.<|14.76|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As we know from having just finished a journey through the liturgical year<|5.12|>
<|5.12|> and having done it many times and getting ready to do it again,<|8.98|>
<|9.60|> we know that we have in Jesus a king who rides on the back of a donkey.<|15.48|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> walks among his people, touches the sick with love and healing hands,<|8.52|>
<|9.66|> eats with the lowliest of society, and is, in fact, enthroned on a cross.<|16.66|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.78|> Our king can be found in the faces of the least of these.<|6.18|>
<|7.32|> And as we follow our shepherd in our good sheep moments,<|11.92|>
<|12.50|> we see him and serve him in each other, in family, in community.<|19.74|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.10|><|endoftext|>
